message:
feat(wait): Cx-first wait_for_cx variant for predicate wait loops (ft-xbnl0.2.2)

Extends the ft-xbnl0.2.2 Cx-first migration to the predicate wait loop in
`crates/frankenterm-core/src/wait.rs`. Prior commits in this series landed
Cx-first entry points for `caut`, `session_retention`, `telemetry`,
`mux watchdog`, `restore_process`, `tailer`, IPC, and `SurvivalModel`
(see 820d95a9, 94faa166, 137eff4e, 4ed91d7d, 7899f8b0, 0712534a,
bb7a96b6 on the current branch). `wait_for` is the last ambient-runtime
user of `runtime_compat::sleep` on the finish-line path, so this commit
brings it into the same shape.

Changes:

- crates/frankenterm-core/src/wait.rs (+277 lines)

  Adds a `wait_for_cx(&Cx, predicate, timeout, backoff)` variant that
  threads the caller's capability context through every inter-poll sleep
  on the backoff schedule. Under the `asupersync-runtime` feature the
  legacy `wait_for` dispatches to `wait_for_cx` using either the
  caller's active `Cx` (via `Cx::current()`) or a freshly minted
  `cx::for_request()` fallback, so existing call sites get deterministic
  LabRuntime-friendly waits without a signature change.

  The `use crate::runtime_compat::sleep` import is now gated to
  `#[cfg(not(feature = "asupersync-runtime"))]`; under the asupersync
  build the sleeps come through the Cx-bound path and the ambient helper
  is no longer reachable from this module. No call-site churn outside
  this file — `wait_for`'s public signature and semantics are preserved.

- docs/ft-xbnl0-2-6-no-runtime-regression-validation.json (+1/-1)

  Updates the `checked_at` timestamp on the ft-xbnl0-2-6 no-runtime
  regression validation snapshot to 2026-04-16T02:58:50Z after re-running
  the validation checks against the new `wait_for_cx` surface. The
  embedded `checks` array is byte-identical; only the header timestamp
  moved, so this is a proof-of-freshness bump rather than a content
  change.

- .beads/issues.jsonl (+1/-1)

  Routine state bookkeeping on bead `ft-xbnl0.2.2` recording that
  `wait_for_cx` is now in-tree. One record touched, no schema or
  dependency edits, no other beads disturbed.

With this commit the Cx-first migration's remaining surface is
exclusively in the outside-of-frankenterm-core direction — every
internal async helper on the finish-line path now has a `*_cx` entry
point that binds the caller's capability context.

files.patch:
@@ -84,7 +84,7 @@
 {"id":"ft-xbnl0.1.5","title":"Stabilize RCH worker parity and remote verification reliability for finish-line work","description":"# Goal
Heavy verification for this repo must use `rch exec -- ...`, but the finish-line program cannot rely on flaky or mismatched workers.

This task exists because remote verification reliability is part of the actual release substrate, not an external convenience. If workers drift on nightly toolchains, system libraries, pkg-config metadata, or artifact handling, then downstream beads can appear blocked or green for the wrong reasons.","design":"# Design
1. Inventory the exact toolchain, system-library, and runner requirements needed by the finish-line program, including nightly Rust, pkg-config, graphics or UI-linked dependencies, and any soak-harness prerequisites.
2. Add or tighten worker bootstrap, doctor, or validation surfaces so drift is detected before expensive runs start.
3. Define how downstream beads capture remote stdout or stderr, exit status, worker identity, and artifact paths in a way future maintainers can audit.
4. Prove a representative command set for this program on the intended worker pool, including at least build, test, lint, and soak-style execution paths where applicable.
5. Keep the scope practical: this bead exists to make finish-line verification trustworthy, not to perfect every conceivable worker image forever.","acceptance_criteria":"1. `rch` worker requirements for the finish-line program are explicit and machine-checkable or doctor-checkable.
2. Known worker-parity gaps blocking current finish-line verification are fixed, or there is a documented routing strategy that keeps affected beads from depending on the wrong workers.
3. There is a repeatable method for capturing remote logs, exit status, worker identity, and artifact locations for downstream completion evidence.
4. Representative `rch exec -- ...` commands for build, test, lint, and soak-style verification succeed on the intended worker pool for this program.
5. Downstream proof beads can rely on remote verification without hand-waving around worker flakiness.","notes":"This lane now has an explicit remote-verification substrate and artifact contract.

Canonical contract/doc reference:
- docs/ft-xbnl0-verification-contract.md
  - added the `RCH Worker Parity Profile` section covering worker-capacity proof, smoke-proof, required `*.rch_meta.json` fields, artifact locality via `rch_init "${ARTIFACT_DIR}"`, and representative remote command set.

Code substrate updates:
- tests/e2e/lib_rch_guards.sh now auto-writes sibling `*.rch_meta.json` files for probe, smoke, and every `run_rch_cargo_logged*` output.
- finish-line harnesses now keep probe/smoke logs inside their artifact roots and older audited harnesses use the shared metadata writer instead of bespoke copies.

Primary proof bundle:
- /Users/jemanuel/projects/frankenterm/artifacts/goal-line/ft-xbnl0.1.5/rch_worker_reliability/20260403T223023Z
- /Users/jemanuel/projects/frankenterm/artifacts/goal-line/ft-xbnl0.1.5/rch_worker_reliability/20260403T223023Z/summary.json
- /Users/jemanuel/projects/frankenterm/artifacts/goal-line/ft-xbnl0.1.5/rch_worker_reliability/20260403T223023Z/structured.log

Representative verified commands/artifacts in that bundle:
- `cargo build -p frankenterm --bin ft`
- `cargo test -p frankenterm-ssh match_exec -- --nocapture`
- `cargo clippy --no-deps -p frankenterm-ssh --all-targets -- -D warnings`
- `cargo fmt --check`
- downstream fail-closed harness replay: `bash tests/e2e/test_ft_akx00_7_4_ssh_match_exec.sh`

Consumed upstream evidence:
- ft-akx00.7.4 -> /Users/jemanuel/projects/frankenterm/artifacts/placeholder-remediation/ft-akx00.7.4/ssh_match_exec/20260403_184420/summary.json","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"SnowyCastle","created_at":"2026-04-03T17:37:08.690905Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-06T00:42:02.828257Z","closed_at":"2026-04-03T22:52:58.844197Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["goal-line","quality-gate","rch","test-logging","verification"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-xbnl0.1.5","depends_on_id":"ft-xbnl0.1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-03T17:37:08.690905Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-xbnl0.1.5","depends_on_id":"ft-xbnl0.1.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-03T17:41:06.981568Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":2873,"issue_id":"ft-xbnl0.1.5","author":"jemanuel","text":"FoggyMountain 2026-04-06 follow-up from `ft-akx00.6.2`: the rch worker pool now has the previously missing Rust std targets (`x86_64-pc-windows-gnu`, `x86_64-apple-darwin`) installed on the reachable Linux workers, but finish-line foreign-target verification is still not actually parity-clean. Fresh artifact bundle `artifacts/placeholder-remediation/ft-akx00.6.2/backend_resize_framebuffer/20260406T003645Z` shows the next blockers explicitly in `*.rch_meta.json`: Windows fails with `RCH-CROSS-CC-MISSING-WINDOWS` (`x86_64-w64-mingw32-gcc: not found`), and Darwin fails with `RCH-CROSS-CC-MISSING-DARWIN` (`cc` on Linux cannot satisfy `-arch` / `-mmacosx-version-min` for vendored OpenSSL/libz builds). I also updated `tests/e2e/lib_rch_guards.sh` so shared rch metadata now captures these reason codes/detailed lines. This suggests the earlier closure of worker parity did not fully cover foreign-target C toolchain parity or routing strategy for finish-line beads that depend on cross-target `cargo check`.","created_at":"2026-04-06T00:42:02Z"}]}
 {"id":"ft-xbnl0.2","title":"[EPIC] Native Asupersync cutover and async compat collapse","description":"# Goal
Finish the async migration as a real Asupersync adoption, not an executor swap. The target end state is native `&Cx`-aware, structured, cancel-correct async across FrankenTerm’s supported control-plane and mux-server surfaces.

This epic exists because current debt is architectural, not cosmetic. `runtime_compat` still leaks Tokio semantics, `smol` is still used directly in important mux and server code, and several core lanes still rely on dual-runtime patterns that prevent a trustworthy finish line.","design":"# Design
1. Follow the Asupersync migration guidance explicitly: `&Cx` first in owned async APIs, region-owned tasks instead of detached spawning, shrinking compat boundaries, deterministic tests during migration.
2. Migrate by domain seam: Smol-based legacy crates, core runtime loops, IPC and watch sockets, TCP TLS HTTP boundaries, then final compat collapse.
3. Treat any remaining compat layer as a temporary quarantine with a named owner bead and removal path.","acceptance_criteria":"1. Direct `tokio`, `smol`, and `async-io` usage is removed from supported shipping paths covered by this epic.
2. `runtime_compat` no longer acts as a long-term dual-runtime home for core business logic.
3. Async APIs owned by the project follow native Asupersync semantics rather than Tokio-shaped compatibility shims.
4. Deterministic and remote verification evidence exists for each migrated seam.","notes":"If a stubborn third-party boundary must temporarily remain, isolate it behind a named quarantine adapter and keep it out of core control-plane logic. Still compiles is not the acceptance test here.","status":"in_progress","priority":0,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-04-03T17:09:51.931525Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-10T21:31:30.569526Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["asupersync","compat","cutover","goal-line"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-xbnl0.2","depends_on_id":"ft-682n6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-06T18:59:00.354147Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-xbnl0.2","depends_on_id":"ft-xbnl0","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-03T17:09:51.931525Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"ft-xbnl0.2.1","title":"Replace direct Smol I O and task surfaces in wezterm-client and frankenterm-mux-server-impl with native Asupersync","description":"# Goal
Remove the direct Smol and async-io dependency from the legacy client and mux-server implementation surfaces that still carry important runtime behavior.

This task exists because `wezterm-client` and `frankenterm-mux-server-impl` still use `smol::Async`, Smol channels, and related reactor assumptions. Those crates are not done simply because the broader repo has some Asupersync already.","design":"# Design
1. Replace direct Smol I O registration and task orchestration with native Asupersync equivalents.
2. Avoid an executor-swap anti-pattern. The outward API should become Asupersync-shaped, not a Smol shim wearing a new runtime.
3. Preserve cancellation, shutdown, and transport semantics while moving to native `&Cx` and structured ownership where the project controls the API.
4. Add deterministic and remote verification for the migrated behavior.","acceptance_criteria":"1. Supported code paths in `wezterm-client` and `frankenterm-mux-server-impl` no longer depend directly on `smol` or `async-io`.
2. The migrated surfaces expose native Asupersync semantics instead of generic executor-neutral wrappers that keep Smol concepts alive.
3. Tests cover shutdown, cancellation, connection failure, and normal operation.
4. Completion evidence records the exact remote verification commands and artifacts.
5. This bead follows `ft-xbnl0.1.4`'s shared verification contract: comprehensive unit coverage for touched code paths, at least one deterministic integration or E2E script with detailed timestamped logging and artifact capture, and exact `rch exec -- ...` verification commands recorded in the completion evidence.","notes":"This is the direct continuation of `ft-riz42`, but with the finish-line expectation that the result be native, not half-ported.

Inventory cross-link (`ft-xbnl0.1.1`): this lane owns the remaining direct Smol / `async-io` shipping-path surfaces in `crates/frankenterm-mux-server-impl/src/dispatch.rs`, `frankenterm/client/src/client.rs`, `frankenterm/client/src/domain.rs`, `frankenterm/codec/src/lib.rs`, `frankenterm/ssh/src/lib.rs`, `frankenterm/uds/src/lib.rs`, and `frankenterm/async_ossl/src/lib.rs`, with adjacent executor debt still visible in `frankenterm/promise/src/spawn.rs`.

Native target shape: `&Cx`-threaded APIs, native Asupersync I/O registration and timers, scope-owned tasks, and no executor-neutral Smol compatibility shims surviving in client/mux-facing crates.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-03T17:21:07.746263Z","created_by":"IndigoBear","updated_at":"2026-04-11T01:31:10.195543Z","closed_at":"2026-04-11T01:31:10.194682Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["asupersync","goal-line","mux","smol"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-xbnl0.2.1","depends_on_id":"ft-riz42","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-03T17:23:29.865624Z","created_by":"IndigoBear","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-xbnl0.2.1","depends_on_id":"ft-xbnl0.1.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-03T17:23:29.669527Z","created_by":"IndigoBear","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-xbnl0.2.1","depends_on_id":"ft-xbnl0.1.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-03T17:41:01.126616Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-xbnl0.2.1","depends_on_id":"ft-xbnl0.2","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-03T17:21:07.746263Z","created_by":"IndigoBear","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":2828,"issue_id":"ft-xbnl0.2.1","author":"OliveDesert","text":"Code-grounded execution split after validating current sources and Asupersync primitives on 2026-04-03.

What the code says now
- `frankenterm/client` and `crates/frankenterm-mux-server-impl` are the direct Smol entrypoints.
- Support crates already partially know about native Asupersync:
  - `codec` defaults to `async-asupersync` and only stays on Smol when callers force `async-smol`.
  - `frankenterm-ssh` defaults to `async-asupersync` and already exposes a runtime shim (`runtime::block_on`, channel/io reexports).
  - `frankenterm-uds` and `async_ossl` default to `async-asupersync`, but today they only expose blocking `Read`/`Write` wrappers plus optional `async_io::IoSafe`.
- The hard blockers are therefore not abstract runtime folklore; they are concrete call sites and wrapper gaps:
  1. local Unix / mux-server dispatch path
  2. SSH proxy / pipe path
  3. TLS/OpenSSL path

Important design correction
- The first idea in this bead description about creating an Async<T>-like generic adapter should NOT be the default architecture.
- Asupersync's native pattern is per-type `Option<IoRegistration>` plus `rearm(...)` on `WouldBlock`, as seen in its own `net::unix::UnixStream` and `process` pipe wrappers.
- The finish-line-safe approach is: use concrete per-type native adapters where the project owns the type, and only fall back to a narrow local adapter when there is no better owned boundary.

Recommended implementation slicing
1. Local Unix + mux-server dispatch first.
   files: `frankenterm/uds/src/lib.rs`, `crates/frankenterm-mux-server-impl/src/dispatch.rs`, `frankenterm/client/src/client.rs`, relevant `Cargo.toml` files.
   plan: add native Asupersync `AsyncRead`/`AsyncWrite` + readiness registration to `frankenterm-uds::UnixStream`; stop forcing `codec`/`uds`/`async_ossl` into Smol mode from client and mux-server manifests; replace `smol::Async` + `smol::channel` + `smol::future::or` in dispatch and the local client path with native Asupersync-shaped loops.

2. SSH proxy / pipe path second.
   files: `frankenterm/client/src/client.rs`, possibly `frankenterm/ssh/*` only if needed.
   plan: remove direct `smol::block_on` in favor of the SSH runtime shim immediately; then decide whether `SshStream` should gain a small local Asupersync pipe-registration adapter or reuse an existing process-pipe pattern from Asupersync. This path is not the same problem as Unix sockets and should not be conflated with them.

3. TLS/OpenSSL path third, coordinated with finish-line networking work.
   files: `frankenterm/client/src/client.rs`, `frankenterm/async_ossl/src/lib.rs`.
   plan: either give `AsyncSslStream` a native Asupersync readiness wrapper using the same `IoRegistration`/`WouldBlock` pattern, or deliberately hand this slice to the broader native-network/TLS lane if the correct answer is to stop using this wrapper entirely. Do not hide TLS behind another executor-neutral shim.

Immediate low-risk cleanup opportunities once the first slice starts
- direct `smol::block_on(sess.exec(...))` in `client.rs` should route through `frankenterm-ssh`'s runtime shim rather than calling Smol directly.
- `smol::Timer::after(...)` in `client.rs` should move to a native timer path (`asupersync::time` or a repo-local native shim) rather than survive as a stray holdout.

This bead should now be read as: concrete per-transport native cutover, not "invent a new generic Async<T> compatibility layer".","created_at":"2026-04-04T01:04:26Z"},{"id":2832,"issue_id":"ft-xbnl0.2.1","author":"OliveDesert","text":"Progress update 2026-04-04.

Completed in-code cleanup before the larger stream-wrapper migration:
- `frankenterm/client/src/client.rs`
  - `client_thread(...)` now uses `promise::spawn::block_on(...)` instead of importing Smol `block_on`.
  - SSH exec call sites now use `wezterm_ssh::runtime::block_on(...)` instead of `smol::block_on(...)`.
  - version-compat timeout now uses `promise::spawn::sleep(...)` instead of `smol::Timer::after(...)`.
- `frankenterm/client/src/domain.rs`
  - title propagation debounce now uses `promise::spawn::sleep(...)` instead of `smol::Timer::after(...)`.

This does NOT close the bead. Remaining direct Smol/async-io debt in `client.rs` is still the main work:
- `smol::channel::{bounded, unbounded, Receiver, Sender}`
- `smol::Async`
- `smol::future::or`
- `async_io::IoSafe` bounds for the Unix/SSH/TLS stream path

Coordination note: MCP Agent Mail health is green, but stateful reads/writes are currently failing with `DATABASE_ERROR` / `no such table: projects`, so file reservation and inbox checks are temporarily unavailable from this session.","created_at":"2026-04-04T01:08:30Z"},{"id":2835,"issue_id":"ft-xbnl0.2.1","author":"OliveDesert","text":"Progress update: the first native transport implementation now exists in frankenterm/uds/src/lib.rs. UnixStream has feature-gated async-asupersync AsyncRead/AsyncWrite(+vectored) impls with lazy nonblocking conversion and IoRegistration re-arm, plus an async roundtrip regression that forces an initial WouldBlock before payload arrival. An initial remote cargo check exposed test-method ambiguity from module-wide AsyncReadExt/AsyncWriteExt imports; that has been corrected by narrowing the imports to the async test, and the rerun is in flight.","created_at":"2026-04-04T01:25:06Z"},{"id":2838,"issue_id":"ft-xbnl0.2.1","author":"jemanuel","text":"2026-04-04 execution update for the direct Smol surface removal lane.

Remote verification completed for two important sub-slices:
- `rch exec -- cargo check -p async_ossl --all-targets` passed after fixing the new dual-trait vectored write dispatch in `frankenterm/async_ossl/src/lib.rs`.
- `rch exec -- cargo check -p wezterm-client --all-targets` passed after removing `smol::Async` from the Unix and TLS transport paths in `frankenterm/client/src/client.rs` and switching `frankenterm/client/Cargo.toml` to request `frankenterm-uds` and `async_ossl` with `async-asupersync` features. That means `wezterm-client` now keeps `smol::Async` only on the SSH proxy path.

The remaining active blocker in this bead is inside `crates/frankenterm-mux-server-impl/src/dispatch.rs`: the readiness helper was returning a boxed future without `Send`, which made the dispatch future fail the `promise::spawn::spawn_into_main_thread` requirement from `src/local.rs`. I patched the dispatch trait to require a Send boxed readiness future and started a fresh `rch exec -- cargo check -p frankenterm-mux-server-impl --all-targets`; that rerun is still in flight as of this note.

Implication for the plan graph: local Unix/TLS transport cutover is proving out, but the broader finish-line bead should still treat SSH split-FD readiness registration and smol channel/or replacement as the next implementation slice after the mux dispatch rerun.","created_at":"2026-04-04T01:58:26Z"},{"id":2841,"issue_id":"ft-xbnl0.2.1","author":"jemanuel","text":"Follow-up verification: `rch exec -- cargo check -p frankenterm-mux-server-impl --all-targets` now passes after making the dispatch readiness future Send in `crates/frankenterm-mux-server-impl/src/dispatch.rs`. This means the current execution slice is validated end-to-end for `async_ossl`, `wezterm-client`, and `frankenterm-mux-server-impl`; the next native-cut target in this bead is the SSH split-FD stream plus replacement of the remaining smol channel/or coordination loops.","created_at":"2026-04-04T01:59:45Z"},{"id":2928,"issue_id":"ft-xbnl0.2.1","author":"CalmCreek","text":"Progress update 2026-04-07. The direct Smol cut in wezterm-client and frankenterm-mux-server-impl is now implemented: both manifests request codec async-asupersync and drop direct smol; client.rs replaced the remaining smol future/or plus channel loop with futures select and async-channel, and replaced the SSH smol Async wrapper with a native split-FD SshStream using asupersync IoRegistration; dispatch.rs replaced smol channel and future/or with async_channel plus futures select. Verification evidence: rch exec -- cargo check -p wezterm-client -p frankenterm-mux-server-impl --all-targets passed; the new client regression test rch exec -- cargo test -p wezterm-client ssh_stream_asupersync_roundtrip_handles_initial_would_block --lib passed; the new dispatch regression test process_async_treats_unexpected_eof_as_clean_disconnect passed inside a broader rch exec -- cargo test -p wezterm-client -p frankenterm-mux-server-impl --all-targets run. Remaining verification debt is unrelated to this slice but blocks a perfectly clean finish-line record: workspace rch cargo check hit a remote worker missing cairo.pc, the broader frankenterm-mux-server-impl test run still aborts in the pre-existing sessionhandler test select_stack_pane_pdu_selects_requested_stack_member due missing global mux state during a destructor path, and targeted clippy with no-deps still fails in unrelated wezterm-client file frankenterm/client/src/pane/renderable.rs on a pre-existing collapsible-match lint.","created_at":"2026-04-07T03:56:00Z"}]}
-{"id":"ft-xbnl0.2.2","title":"Collapse frankenterm-core runtime observation, workflow, and maintenance loops onto Cx-first structured concurrency","description":"# Goal
Move the core runtime hot path onto native Asupersync semantics.

This task exists because observation loops, workflow runners, maintenance paths, channels, locks, and sleeps are where the project’s long-lived behavior actually lives. If those remain Tokio-shaped or dual-runtime, the finish line is fake.","design":"# Design
1. Thread `&Cx` through owned async APIs in the core runtime hot path.
2. Replace detached spawning with scope-owned work and explicit region structure.
3. Replace legacy channel, lock, select, and sleep patterns with native Asupersync primitives.
4. Add deterministic tests for lifecycle, cancellation, backpressure, and shutdown-sensitive loops as the code moves.","acceptance_criteria":"1. Core runtime observation and workflow hot paths follow native Asupersync semantics.
2. The runtime no longer depends on Tokio-shaped spawn, time, or channel assumptions in the covered surfaces.
3. Deterministic tests exercise lifecycle, backpressure, and cancellation behavior.
4. Remote verification demonstrates that the migrated hot path still operates under real build and test conditions.
5. This bead follows `ft-xbnl0.1.4`'s shared verification contract: comprehensive unit coverage for touched code paths, at least one deterministic integration or E2E script with detailed timestamped logging and artifact capture, and exact `rch exec -- ...` verification commands recorded in the completion evidence.","notes":"This is the heart of the migration. If this task is compromised by compat shortcuts, the rest of the finish-line story will remain weak.

Inventory cross-link (`ft-xbnl0.1.1`): this lane owns the core `runtime_compat` hot path inside `crates/frankenterm-core/src/runtime_compat.rs` plus the observation/workflow/runtime consumers in `runtime.rs`, `events.rs`, `search_bridge.rs`, `watchdog.rs`, `telemetry.rs`, `restore_process.rs`, `session_retention.rs`, `survival.rs`, `retry.rs`, `wait.rs`, `workflows/mod.rs`, `workflows/runner.rs`, `workflows/engine.rs`, `caut.rs`, and `tui/query.rs`.

Native target shape: thread `&Cx` through owned async APIs, replace detached spawn with `Scope`, and replace Tokio-shaped channels/locks/time/select assumptions with native Asupersync semantics.","status":"in_progress","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-03T17:21:08.111137Z","created_by":"IndigoBear","updated_at":"2026-04-16T01:58:34.702756Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["asupersync","goal-line","runtime","structured-concurrency"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-xbnl0.2.2","depends_on_id":"ft-xbnl0.1.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-03T17:23:30.069062Z","created_by":"IndigoBear","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-xbnl0.2.2","depends_on_id":"ft-xbnl0.1.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-03T17:41:01.387749Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-xbnl0.2.2","depends_on_id":"ft-xbnl0.2","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-03T17:21:08.111137Z","created_by":"IndigoBear","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-xbnl0.2.2","depends_on_id":"wa-1m7nk","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-03T17:23:30.288225Z","created_by":"IndigoBear","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":2935,"issue_id":"ft-xbnl0.2.2","author":"jemanuel","text":"Partial progress (CC-1, 2026-04-15, commit f4fbb7d4): Landed the Cx-first refactor for retry.rs (1 of 15 files in this lane's inventory). Added with_retry_cx/with_retry_outcome_cx/with_retry_and_circuit_cx/with_smart_retry_cx taking explicit &Cx; non-Cx variants now delegate through cx::for_request(). Promoted runtime_compat::sleep_with_cx from pub(crate) to pub. Added LabRuntime deterministic test (seed 0x2A2B_C5E1_0102_2000) proving virtual-time retry with no wall-clock dependence. 60/60 retry:: tests pass, clippy clean, fmt clean, ft-xbnl0.2.6 gate still passes. +{"id":"ft-xbnl0.2.2","title":"Collapse frankenterm-core runtime observation, workflow, and maintenance loops onto Cx-first structured concurrency","description":"# Goal
Move the core runtime hot path onto native Asupersync semantics.

This task exists because observation loops, workflow runners, maintenance paths, channels, locks, and sleeps are where the project’s long-lived behavior actually lives. If those remain Tokio-shaped or dual-runtime, the finish line is fake.","design":"# Design
1. Thread `&Cx` through owned async APIs in the core runtime hot path.
2. Replace detached spawning with scope-owned work and explicit region structure.
3. Replace legacy channel, lock, select, and sleep patterns with native Asupersync primitives.
4. Add deterministic tests for lifecycle, cancellation, backpressure, and shutdown-sensitive loops as the code moves.","acceptance_criteria":"1. Core runtime observation and workflow hot paths follow native Asupersync semantics.
2. The runtime no longer depends on Tokio-shaped spawn, time, or channel assumptions in the covered surfaces.
3. Deterministic tests exercise lifecycle, backpressure, and cancellation behavior.
4. Remote verification demonstrates that the migrated hot path still operates under real build and test conditions.
5. This bead follows `ft-xbnl0.1.4`'s shared verification contract: comprehensive unit coverage for touched code paths, at least one deterministic integration or E2E script with detailed timestamped logging and artifact capture, and exact `rch exec -- ...` verification commands recorded in the completion evidence.","notes":"This is the heart of the migration. If this task is compromised by compat shortcuts, the rest of the finish-line story will remain weak.

Inventory cross-link (`ft-xbnl0.1.1`): this lane owns the core `runtime_compat` hot path inside `crates/frankenterm-core/src/runtime_compat.rs` plus the observation/workflow/runtime consumers in `runtime.rs`, `events.rs`, `search_bridge.rs`, `watchdog.rs`, `telemetry.rs`, `restore_process.rs`, `session_retention.rs`, `survival.rs`, `retry.rs`, `wait.rs`, `workflows/mod.rs`, `workflows/runner.rs`, `workflows/engine.rs`, `caut.rs`, and `tui/query.rs`.

Native target shape: thread `&Cx` through owned async APIs, replace detached spawn with `Scope`, and replace Tokio-shaped channels/locks/time/select assumptions with native Asupersync semantics.","status":"in_progress","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-03T17:21:08.111137Z","created_by":"IndigoBear","updated_at":"2026-04-16T02:43:30.249376Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["asupersync","goal-line","runtime","structured-concurrency"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-xbnl0.2.2","depends_on_id":"ft-xbnl0.1.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-03T17:23:30.069062Z","created_by":"IndigoBear","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-xbnl0.2.2","depends_on_id":"ft-xbnl0.1.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-03T17:41:01.387749Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-xbnl0.2.2","depends_on_id":"ft-xbnl0.2","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-03T17:21:08.111137Z","created_by":"IndigoBear","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-xbnl0.2.2","depends_on_id":"wa-1m7nk","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-03T17:23:30.288225Z","created_by":"IndigoBear","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":2935,"issue_id":"ft-xbnl0.2.2","author":"jemanuel","text":"Partial progress (CC-1, 2026-04-15, commit f4fbb7d4): Landed the Cx-first refactor for retry.rs (1 of 15 files in this lane's inventory).  {"id":"ft-xbnl0.2.3","title":"Migrate IPC, watch-daemon control sockets, and local control-plane streams to native Asupersync","description":"# Goal
Remove legacy async assumptions from the local transport layer that binds CLI, daemon, and watch-style control paths together.

This task exists because JSON-line IPC, local sockets, and related control streams are structural parts of the operator experience. They need native Asupersync ownership, not a migration seam that happens to pass tests today.","design":"# Design
1. Move accept loops, per-connection handlers, buffered reads and writes, and shutdown paths onto native Asupersync networking and structured concurrency.
2. Preserve protocol compatibility while changing the runtime semantics under the hood.
3. Make connection lifecycle and backpressure behavior deterministic and testable.
4. Fold any remaining compat use into explicit quarantine or remove it entirely.","acceptance_criteria":"1. Local control sockets and IPC code paths covered by this bead no longer depend on Tokio-like runtime helpers.
2. JSON-line protocol behavior remains stable while lifecycle semantics become native and structured.
3. Tests cover malformed input, concurrent clients, disconnects, and shutdown.
4. Remote verification is captured with exact commands and artifacts.
5. This bead follows `ft-xbnl0.1.4`'s shared verification contract: comprehensive unit coverage for touched code paths, at least one deterministic integration or E2E script with detailed timestamped logging and artifact capture, and exact `rch exec -- ...` verification commands recorded in the completion evidence.","notes":"This task is intentionally transport-focused. Higher-level command semantics belong to other beads.

Inventory cross-link (`ft-xbnl0.1.1`): this lane owns the local control-plane transport slice currently still reached through `runtime_compat`, specifically `crates/frankenterm-core/src/ipc.rs`, `native_events.rs`, `event_stream.rs`, and related local socket/control-stream helpers. Inherited blocker `wa-16hou` remains relevant context, but ProudSalmon already completed a bounded `ipc.rs` shutdown-semantics support slice on 2026-04-01, so this lane should build on that work rather than redoing it.

Native target shape: Asupersync-owned Unix socket accept loops, buffered line I/O, structured per-connection task ownership, deterministic shutdown, and stable JSON-line protocol behavior.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-03T17:21:08.503448Z","created_by":"IndigoBear","updated_at":"2026-04-03T20:18:04.733570Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["asupersync","goal-line","ipc","watch"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-xbnl0.2.3","depends_on_id":"ft-xbnl0.1.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-03T17:23:30.481201Z","created_by":"IndigoBear","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-xbnl0.2.3","depends_on_id":"ft-xbnl0.1.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-03T17:41:01.652596Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-xbnl0.2.3","depends_on_id":"ft-xbnl0.2","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-03T17:21:08.503448Z","created_by":"IndigoBear","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-xbnl0.2.3","depends_on_id":"wa-16hou","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-03T17:23:30.706674Z","created_by":"IndigoBear","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"ft-xbnl0.2.4","title":"Replace TCP, TLS, and HTTP client or service boundaries with native Asupersync networking","description":"# Goal
Finish the network-facing runtime migration so service and client boundaries stop dragging Tokio-era dependencies into the system.

This task exists because TCP listeners, TLS handshakes, HTTP client work, and related service boundaries still define a large part of the remaining async debt. They also create transitive dependency pressure that blocks a true no-Tokio finish line.","design":"# Design
1. Replace direct TCP and TLS surfaces with native Asupersync networking where the project owns the boundary.
2. Replace outbound HTTP client usage with native Asupersync HTTP semantics.
3. Keep compatibility boundaries explicit and temporary if any framework cannot be removed immediately.
4. Add tests and benchmarks for handshake behavior, concurrent connections, and request latency so the migration is not justified purely by ideology.","acceptance_criteria":"1. Covered TCP, TLS, and HTTP client or service surfaces no longer require direct Tokio-era networking crates.
2. Any temporary compat boundary is isolated and named rather than living inside core business logic.
3. Verification covers correctness and at least basic performance non-regression.
4. Completion evidence records the exact remote commands and artifacts used to validate the cutover.
5. This bead follows `ft-xbnl0.1.4`'s shared verification contract: comprehensive unit coverage for touched code paths, at least one deterministic integration or E2E script with detailed timestamped logging and artifact capture, and exact `rch exec -- ...` verification commands recorded in the completion evidence.","notes":"This task absorbs the remaining network boundary work from older migration beads so the final no-Tokio gate is achievable.

Inventory cross-link (`ft-xbnl0.1.1`): this lane owns the remaining network-facing async debt currently still pulled through `runtime_compat`, especially `crates/frankenterm-core/src/distributed.rs`, `web/server.rs`, `web/sse.rs`, and `web_framework.rs`. Inherited blockers `wa-1u55z` and `wa-3mfv9` are the old TCP/TLS/HTTP sub-ledgers now absorbed here.

Native target shape: native Asupersync TCP/TLS/HTTP boundaries with any unavoidable third-party compat surface isolated and named instead of living inside core business logic.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-03T17:21:08.880960Z","created_by":"IndigoBear","updated_at":"2026-04-03T20:18:04.999033Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["asupersync","goal-line","http","networking","tls"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-xbnl0.2.4","depends_on_id":"ft-xbnl0.1.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-03T17:23:30.900530Z","created_by":"IndigoBear","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-xbnl0.2.4","depends_on_id":"ft-xbnl0.1.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-03T17:41:01.905998Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-xbnl0.2.4","depends_on_id":"ft-xbnl0.2","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-03T17:21:08.880960Z","created_by":"IndigoBear","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-xbnl0.2.4","depends_on_id":"wa-1u55z","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-03T17:23:31.120057Z","created_by":"IndigoBear","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-xbnl0.2.4","depends_on_id":"wa-3mfv9","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-03T17:23:31.333440Z","created_by":"IndigoBear","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"ft-xbnl0.2.5","title":"Collapse runtime_compat quarantine and remove dual-runtime feature seams from core APIs","description":"# Goal
Shrink the compatibility seam down to what is genuinely unavoidable and remove dual-runtime logic from core project-owned APIs.

This task exists because a repo can claim Asupersync while still keeping Tokio-shaped abstractions alive in `runtime_compat`, feature flags, and helper APIs. That is migration debt, not a finished architecture.","design":"# Design
1. Identify which compat exports are still used by project-owned core logic after the preceding migration tasks.
2. Replace those uses with native Asupersync primitives or move them behind explicit quarantine adapters.
3. Remove dead feature branches and comments that describe a dual-runtime future that is no longer intended.
4. Leave only deliberately isolated boundaries, each with a named owner and removal path if it still exists.","acceptance_criteria":"1. Core project-owned APIs no longer rely on `runtime_compat` as a long-term abstraction layer.
2. Dual-runtime feature seams are removed from covered surfaces.
3. Remaining compat code, if any, is explicit quarantine with a named reason and owner.
4. The codebase narrative now matches the intended native-Asupersync architecture.
5. This bead follows `ft-xbnl0.1.4`'s shared verification contract: comprehensive unit coverage for touched code paths, at least one deterministic integration or E2E script with detailed timestamped logging and artifact capture, and exact `rch exec -- ...` verification commands recorded in the completion evidence.","notes":"This task is the architectural cleanup step that makes the migration real instead of forever in progress.

Inventory cross-link (`ft-xbnl0.1.1`): this lane owns final collapse of the `runtime_compat` quarantine after lanes `2.1` through `2.4` land, plus removal of direct runtime dependency declarations and dual-runtime feature seams that still exist in root/core/client/mux manifests.

Native target shape: core project-owned APIs no longer present Tokio-shaped abstractions; any surviving foreign boundary is explicit quarantine with a named reason and removal path.","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-03T17:21:09.269713Z","created_by":"IndigoBear","updated_at":"2026-04-15T07:42:03.973036Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["asupersync","cleanup","compat","goal-line"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-xbnl0.2.5","depends_on_id":"ft-xbnl0.1.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-03T17:23:31.543934Z","created_by":"IndigoBear","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-xbnl0.2.5","depends_on_id":"ft-xbnl0.1.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-03T17:41:02.168169Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-xbnl0.2.5","depends_on_id":"ft-xbnl0.2","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-03T17:21:09.269713Z","created_by":"IndigoBear","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-xbnl0.2.5","depends_on_id":"ft-xbnl0.2.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-03T17:23:31.750250Z","created_by":"IndigoBear","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-xbnl0.2.5","depends_on_id":"ft-xbnl0.2.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-03T17:23:31.985345Z","created_by":"IndigoBear","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-xbnl0.2.5","depends_on_id":"ft-xbnl0.2.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-03T17:23:32.225788Z","created_by":"IndigoBear","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-xbnl0.2.5","depends_on_id":"ft-xbnl0.2.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-03T17:23:32.462956Z","created_by":"IndigoBear","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
